items.title: The best, worst offseason moves of the AFC: Barnwe...
items.body_text: The Steelers signing Jamel Dean? Solid move. The Colts guaranteeing big money to Daniel Jones? Not so much. Let's pick out offseason highs and lows in the AFC.

items.title: Eagles LB Smith arrested after car clocked at 135
items.body_text: Eagles linebacker Nolan Smith Jr. was arrested Friday and accused of speeding and reckless driving in Georgia, the Twiggs County Sheriff's Office told ESPN.

items.body_text: Steelers quarterback Aaron Rodgers said Wednesday that the 2026 season will be the last of his Hall of Fame-worthy career.

items.title: Browns HC praises Sanders: 'Come a long way'
items.body_text: Browns coach Todd Monken praised quarterback Shedeur Sanders' development Wednesday, saying the second-year player has "come a long way."

items.title: Raiders' Kubiak: Mendoza has been 'as advertised'
items.body_text: QB Fernando Mendoza has made a strong impression in his first month with the Raiders, with coach Klint Kubiak saying the No. 1 pick has been "as advertised."

items.title: Burrow: 2026 Bengals 'most talented' in my era
items.body_text: Quarterback Joe Burrow said he believes this year's Bengals squad is the most talented roster he's played with since joining the franchise.

items.title: Colts' Ward 'whole' again after tragedy, injuries
items.body_text: After two seasons of heartbreak and injury, Colts cornerback Charvarius Ward, who pondered retirement, said he opted to return because he's in a better headspace.

items.title: Titans hire Gardi as executive VP of football ops
items.body_text: The Titans hired Dave Gardi, formerly of the Commanders, to be their executive vice president of football operations.

items.title: Chiefs' Rice violates probation, ordered to jail
items.body_text: Chiefs receiver Rashee Rice has been sentenced to immediately serve 30 days in jail after testing positive for marijuana in violation of the terms of his probation.
